transcript_facts:
* The patient reports ongoing pain in his right knee.
* The patient reports the knee is still swollen.
* The patient reports the whole leg is now swollen.
* The patient reports pain level of 3/10.
* The patient reports typical pain level of 6/10.
* The patient reports the swelling started about two months ago.
* The patient reports compression socks have not been helping.
* The patient reports pain when straightening the knee.
* The patient reports a sore feeling, like an old bruise.
* The patient reports the knee feels hot.
* The patient reports pitting around the knee and ankle.
* The patient reports normal capillary refill in the toes.
* The patient reports taking xeljanz 11 milligrams daily.
* The patient reports taking celebrex 200mg daily.
* The clinician plans a steroid injection.
* The clinician plans an x-ray of the knee.
* The clinician plans physical therapy.
* The clinician plans a surgery consult.
* The clinician orders CMP level checks every two months.
* The clinician advises reducing salt intake.
* The clinician advises elevating feet.
* The clinician advises following up with the patient’s private care doctor.
